entities.name: BIOECONOMIC MODELLING
entities.description: A multiperiod household-level model used to explore economic and environmental impacts of resource management.

entities.name: RESOURCE QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of natural resources which has feedback effects on land productivity.

entities.name: LAND PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of land.

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to manage natural resources sustainably.

entities.name: POLICIES
entities.description: Government or organizational guidelines influencing resource management.

entities.name: LAND SCARCITY
entities.description: The limited availability of land for agricultural use.

entities.name: ASSET POVERTY
entities.description: Lack of vital assets such as oxen and labour.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY INVESTMENTS
entities.description: Investments made to ensure long-term environmental and economic viability.

entities.name: ANDIT TID
entities.description: A specific area in the central highlands of Ethiopia.

entities.name: OXEN
entities.description: Livestock used as a vital asset for farming.

entities.name: LABOUR
entities.description: Human effort used in farming activities.

entities.name: LAND AND WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices aimed at the sustainable use of land and water resources.

entities.name: WELFARE
entities.description: The economic well-being of the household.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS
entities.description: The effects of technologies and policies on the environment.

entities.name: CONSERVATION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technologies designed to conserve natural resources and prevent degradation.

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality and productivity.
